contextual.title: Overnight Weight Loss and Sleep Structure
contextual.text:
Summary: The objectives of the present study are:~to evaluate if overnight weight loss is dependent on sleep structure assessed by polysomnography;~to compare weight loss rate during sleep and awake rest;~to compare night weight loss profile of normal and obese volunteers.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 normal and obese volunteers 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 serious medical or neurological condition

Obesity.definition: The amount of fat or lipid deposit at a site or an organ in the body, an indicator of body fat status.
